term: hit the nail on the head
definition: To describe exactly what is causing a situation or problem; to be precisely correct.
usage_notes: Used to affirm someone else's accurate observation
Always positive — a compliment on precision

etymology.origin_summary: From carpentry — hitting a nail squarely on its head drives it in cleanly. Known since at least the 16th century.

examples: You hit the nail on the head — the root cause was the configuration change.
Her diagnosis hit the nail on the head.

confusions.difference: 'On the nose' means exactly right (often quantities); 'hit the nail on the head' implies identifying the core issue.

literal_gloss: Strike a nail on its flat top so it drives in straight.
figurative_meaning: Identify the exact issue or give a perfectly accurate answer.
common_contexts: discussions where someone identifies a root cause
misuse_notes: Not appropriate when the insight is only partially correct.
